message:
feat(net): worker_id provenance in WorkerEnvelope to prevent stale-session poisoning

WorkerEnvelope gains an optional worker_id field that carries the
worker runtime instance identity for worker-originated messages. Main-
thread/coordinator messages leave this unset via the existing new()
constructor. Worker-originated messages use two new constructors:
from_worker() and from_worker_with_decision_seq() that require a
worker_id string.

replay_hash now incorporates the worker_id into its deterministic
digest via FNV-style byte mixing, ensuring that messages from different
worker instances produce distinct hashes even when all other fields
match. This prevents a replaced worker's stale in-flight messages from
satisfying replay validation for the new session.

The field is serde-optional with skip_serializing_if to maintain wire
compatibility with older coordinators that don't send worker_id.

State machine updates from previous commit (cancel-before-running,
completion-races-cancel) are included with additional tests for the
coordinator accepting completion during CancelRequested state and
allowing cancel from Queued state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
